subject: Network Discovery callback URL is incorrect
commit_message:
Network Discovery callback URL is incorrect

Issue-ID: LOG-700

The Network Discovery Context Builder should use the serviceName when
building the callback URL for Network Discovery Micro Servie.

Make this configurable.

Change-Id: I97ed8e78b006ff5d587beedcefc577701c0d33b9
Signed-off-by: Tait,Trevor(rt0435) <rtait@amdocs.com>

Patch Set 1:

I did the calls from inside the robot container – and did not try without the “onap” – like goera says it should work without – I’ll also test
It may complicate the code because the “onap” namespace will need to be passed in from a helm variable – as it is not even in the yamls but is runtime.

For the demo I would go with what works and retrofit the code or raise a jira for the best practice later

Patch Set 1:

should be ok - geora got me worried about different demo/default namespaces - we can pickup the namespace either way like

LOBAL_INJECTED_POMBA_NETWORK_DISC_CONTEXT_BUILDER_IP_ADDR = "pomba-networkdiscoveryctxbuilder.{{include "common.namespace" .}}"

Patch Set 1:

The oom change is still pending.

Patch Set 1:

A better pattern would be to merge oom always first - then do application changes - otherwise healthcheck will fail

post container and healthcheck status in a clean view in the future
also consult kibana.onap.info and jenkins.onap.info for CD results

Patch Set 1:

can you link the OOM changes - so we can fix this ASAP - or back out this change

Patch Set 1:

Was advised on the issue - turns out that the pomba side was changed without waiting for the OOM config side to merge - and left for the weekend.
In the future all merges to OOM should be put in first and only then put in application changes

going forward
- post healthcheck results and containers up for pomba in your review

Patch Set 1:

Michael, if we put OOM changes first without application changes - it is going to fail as well. They are really coupled. So, we need to come up with some solution that will make sure both OOM and application CI are merged at the same time

Patch Set 1:

sometimes if there is is a global config that would bust other pods - in that case either use 2 variables if possible - a versioned approach - or get the pomba review ready to merge and coordinate with Mandeep or Mike or alexis to merge in a 30 min window - I had the same thing happen in reverse the other day - my oom config was merged first before testsuite - the full fix is to move to git symlinks where oom config is in the pomba repo - will talk with Mike again on this
